titleStmt.title: Letter from C. J. Hyder to Sir Matthew Nathan, 8 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Charles John Hyder

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Charles John Hyde (b. 1862), the manager of W & A Gilbey Ltd. to Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), Undersecretary for Ireland. Mathew Nathan had become undersecretary in 1914 under Augustine Birrell (1850–1933). After the Easter Rising 1916, Nathan resigned as undersecretary due to his misjudgement and failure to stop the Rising. The letter states that Hyde received a letter from Lord Blythe (d. 1925) the director of W & A Gilbey Ltd, a wine importers and distillers. The letter stated that he offered the government room in the company for postal services due to the disturbance of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5611/7970/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
W. & A. Gilbey. Ltd Wine Importers & Distillers DUBLIN 8th May, 1916. Sir, Our Lord Blyth writes us a letter dated the 3rd ins, received
this morning, to say that he had offered the Government the use of the
upper portion of our house here, if required, for Postal or other service
during the present crisis. At Lord Blyths' request we write to confirm this, as we can
give you the use of several large rooms, and will give you every facility
possible if you would think well of accepting our offer. Yours respectfully, P.Pro. W. & A. Gilbey Ltd.
C. J. Hyder Manager
Sir Matthew Natha, K.C.M.G. DUBLIN CASTLE
